<findings>

[observation] [info] Anthropic tool definitions live exclusively in `packages/hench/src/tools/dispatch.ts:1`, so register any new automation there to inherit GuardError handling instead of bypassing the dispatcher.
[observation] [info] GuardRails in `packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts:1` already enforces path, command, and git allowlists, so thread any new tool operations through this facade to keep policy enforcement and auditing centralized.
[observation] [info] Session-aware limits in `packages/hench/src/guard/policy.ts:1` log every allow/deny decision; review or persist that audit log before raising rate/byte caps so you don’t lose visibility into abusive sequences.

</findings>

<insights>

- GuardRails centralizes command/path/git validation along with audit logging.
- Tool dispatcher is the single interface Anthropics tool-calls can reach, keeping GuardError handling uniform.
- GuardRails in `packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts:1` already enforces path, command, and git allowlists, so thread any new tool operations through this facade to keep policy enforcement and auditing centralized.
- Session-aware limits in `packages/hench/src/guard/policy.ts:1` log every allow/deny decision; review or persist that audit log before raising rate/byte caps so you don’t lose visibility into abusive sequences.
- Anthropic tool definitions live exclusively in `packages/hench/src/tools/dispatch.ts:1`, so register any new automation there to inherit GuardError handling instead of bypassing the dispatcher.
- [call graph] 198 internal calls, 27 outgoing, 2 incoming (cohesion: 0.88, coupling: 0.12)

</insights>
